question_title: Will Nintendo officially announce a Legend of Zelda: Ocarina of Time remake/remaster for Nintendo Switch 2 before June 14, 2026?
summary: This question requires two conditions: (1) a Nintendo Direct must occur before June 14, 2026, and (2) it must include an OoT remake announcement. Multiple credible insiders converge on a June 9 Direct (~78% probability within window). Conditional on the Direct happening, the probability of OoT being featured is moderate (~52%) — it's heavily rumored and fits the 40th anniversary, but Nintendo might save it for a dedicated event. The conditional decomposition yields P(YES) ≈ 0.78 × 0.52 + 0.03 (other channels) ≈ 0.44. The factor logit sum implies a higher ~61%, but the multiplicative AND-structure of the question makes the conditional decomposition more appropriate. The main upside risk is that insider accuracy proves even higher than estimated; the main downside risk is the Direct slipping past June 13.

factors.description: Multiple credible insiders (Jeff Grubb, Nate the Hate) converge on a Nintendo Direct in the 'second week of June' / specifically June 9 — well within the resolution window ending June 13
Nate the Hate's Star Fox leak proved fully accurate (announced May 6, releasing June 25), boosting credibility of his parallel OoT remake claim
2026 is Zelda's 40th anniversary — strong strategic motivation for Nintendo to announce a major Zelda title at a summer Direct
Holiday 2026 release target for OoT remake (per leakers) means announcement timing aligns naturally with a summer Direct
No official Nintendo confirmation of any June Direct yet — Nintendo has not committed and could delay beyond June 13
Even if a Direct happens, OoT might not be shown — Nintendo could save it for a dedicated Zelda Direct or later event (Nate said it 'could provide our first look', not 'will')
Status quo is NO: no announcement exists. Question requires two uncertain conditions (Direct happening + OoT being featured) to be jointly met
Earlier speculation pointed to June 16 (after resolution cutoff) as a Direct date — some risk the timing falls just outside the window

reasoning:
**Final Forecast: 0.44 (44%)**

**Key reasoning:**
- **Two conditions must be met**: A Nintendo Direct must happen AND it must feature the OoT remake announcement — both before June 14
- **P(Direct before June 14) ≈ 78%**: Strong insider convergence on June 9, but Nintendo hasn't officially confirmed anything yet
- **P(OoT shown | Direct) ≈ 52%**: Heavily rumored and fits the Zelda 40th anniversary, but Nintendo might save it for a dedicated reveal
- **Combined: ~44%**, accounting for a small additional probability from non-Direct channels
- The tight resolution window (ending June 13) and the compound nature of the question keep this below 50% despite strong insider signals
sources_consulted: [(Metaculus) Will Nintendo officially announce a Legend of Zelda: Ocarina of Time remake/remaster for Nintendo Switch 2 before June 14, 2026?](https://www.metaculus.com/questions/43772)
[Legend of Zelda Remake Reveal Teased For 4 June](https://www.gamingbible.com/news/platform/nintendo/legend-of-zelda-remake-reveal-teased-for-june-071314-20260601)
[Next Nintendo Direct Will Reportedly Happen Coming Tuesday On June 9, 2026](https://twistedvoxel.com/nintendo-direct-happens-coming-tuesday-june-9-2026/)
[Rumour: Nintendo Direct June 2026 Will Broadcast Next Week | Nintendo Insider](https://www.nintendo-insider.com/rumour-nintendo-direct-june-2026-will-broadcast-next-week/)
resolution_criteria:
This question resolves YES if, at any time between May 31, 2026 and 23:59 UTC on June 13, 2026, Nintendo officially announces a new remake, remaster, or otherwise re-released version of The Legend of Zelda: Ocarina of Time for the Nintendo Switch 2 platform. "Officially announce" means a public reveal by Nintendo Co., Ltd. or one of its regional subsidiaries (Nintendo of America, Nintendo of Europe, etc.) via any of the following: a Nintendo Direct or Nintendo-hosted video presentation, the official Nintendo website (nintendo.com), the official Zelda website (zelda.com), Nintendo's official social media accounts, or an official Nintendo press release. The announcement must explicitly identify the game as Ocarina of Time (or include in-game footage/imagery unambiguously identifying it as Ocarina of Time) and confirm it is coming to Nintendo Switch 2.

Resolution will primarily be based on listings on Nintendo's official Direct archive (https://www.nintendo.com/us/nintendo-direct/) and the official Zelda website (https://www.zelda.com/), supplemented by reporting from [credible sources](https://www.metaculus.com/faq/#definitions). Resolves NO if no such official Nintendo announcement has occurred by 23:59 UTC on June 13, 2026.
fine_print:
- Leaks, rumors, insider claims, or unofficial reporting (e.g., from Nate the Hate, Jeff Grubb, or other journalists/leakers) do NOT count, no matter how credible — only an official reveal by Nintendo itself satisfies the criteria.
- Any form of new Switch 2 release of Ocarina of Time counts (full remake, remaster, port, Nintendo Switch Online N64 addition newly announced in this window, or compilation that includes it), provided Nintendo officially announces it during the window.
- The previously released Nintendo Switch Online + Expansion Pack N64 version of Ocarina of Time (already available before this question's window) does NOT count on its own; the announcement must be of a new/separate Switch 2 release or product offering.
- If Nintendo's official Direct page or zelda.com is temporarily unavailable, archived video of an official Nintendo presentation or an official Nintendo press release is sufficient evidence.

No official Nintendo confirmation of any June Direct yet — Nintendo has not committed, could delay beyond June 13

input.anchor: Base rate for any specific game being announced in a ~2 week window when heavily rumored by credible insiders: roughly 35-40%. Leaker-backed rumors with corroborating evidence resolve positively about 60-70% of the time, but the tight window and two-condition requirement pulls this down.
input.assessment: This question requires TWO things to happen: (1) a Nintendo Direct must occur before June 14, and (2) that Direct must include an OoT remake announcement. Multiple credible insiders converge on a June 9 Direct (~78% likely within window). Even if the Direct happens, OoT being shown is uncertain (~52%) — Nintendo might save it for a dedicated Zelda Direct, or focus on other titles. The combined probability is approximately 44%. The main upside risk is that the insider consensus proves fully correct (both Direct date and OoT content). The main downside risk is that the Direct slips past June 13, or occurs but doesn't include OoT.
input.calibration_notes: Decomposed into conditional chain: P(Direct) × P(OoT|Direct). The 44% estimate avoids hedging toward 50% — it reflects genuine two-condition uncertainty. The anchor divergence is small (from ~37% base to ~44% with evidence). Status quo resolution is NO. No double-counting between Star Fox credibility and insider convergence — Star Fox informs the credibility weighting, not the Direct timing.
input.key_uncertainties: 1. Whether Nintendo will actually hold a Direct before June 14 (vs. June 16+ as some earlier speculation suggested). 2. Whether OoT remake would be shown at a general Direct or saved for a separate event. 3. Whether the game is far enough along to show publicly.
input.tool_audit: Research sub-agent provided solid background from Wikipedia but lacked real-time search on one query. Web search tools returned very useful results with multiple corroborating articles. Fetch_url successfully extracted key details from GamingBible, TwistedVoxel, and Nintendo-Insider. Nintendo's official Direct archive confirmed no June 2026 Direct listed yet. All tools functioned well.
input.update_triggers: 1. Nintendo officially announces a Direct date before June 14 → P(YES) jumps to ~55-60%. 2. Nintendo announces a Direct for June 14+ → P(YES) drops to ~5%. 3. A new credible leak confirms OoT as part of the June Direct lineup → P(YES) rises to ~60-65%.
input.process_reflection: The research tools worked well for this question. The decomposition into P(Direct) × P(OoT|Direct) was natural and helped avoid narrative reasoning. The main friction was that one research sub-agent had tools unavailable, but the other compensated well. The web search results were very informative and up-to-date.

revision_history.reviewer_assessment:
## Verification Summary

### Resolution Criteria
Resolution criteria are fully provided and correctly engaged. The agent properly distinguishes between insider rumors (which do NOT count for resolution) and official Nintendo announcements. The May 31 – June 13, 2026 window is correctly identified and all factors operate within this constraint.

### Factor-by-Factor Verification

**Factor 1 (Insider convergence on June 9 Direct, logit=+1.2):** Grounded. Trace confirms Jeff Grubb said "Tuesday, June 9, 2026" via TwistedVoxel fetch, and Nate the Hate said "second week of June" via Nintendo-Insider fetch. Multiple web search results corroborate. ✓

**Factor 2 (Star Fox leak validates Nate the Hate's credibility, logit=+0.8):** Grounded. Research output confirms Star Fox was announced via surprise Direct on May 6/7, 2026. CBR article explicitly links the Star Fox leak accuracy to OoT remake credibility. ✓

**Factor 3 (Zelda 40th anniversary, logit=+0.6):** Grounded. GameRant snippet: "2026 is the 40th anniversary of The Legend of Zelda franchise." ✓

**Factor 4 (Holiday 2026 release target per leakers, logit=+0.5):** Grounded. TwistedVoxel fetch confirms OoT is "rumored as a major holiday release." ✓

**Factor 5 (No official Direct confirmation, logit=-0.8):** Grounded. Nintendo Direct archive page fetch confirmed no June 2026 Direct listed. Research output confirms "no Nintendo Direct for June 2026 has been officially announced." ✓

**Factor 6 (OoT might not be shown even if Direct happens, logit=-0.7):** Reasonable inference. Nate the Hate's wording was "could provide our first look" — speculative, not definitive. Logit sign correct. ✓

**Factor 7 (Status quo / two-condition requirement, logit=-0.5):** Correctly reflects the AND-structure of the question. ✓

**Factor 8 (June 16 speculation, logit=-0.6):** Grounded. Question background mentions "some speculation pointing to dates like June 16, 2026 — which would fall AFTER this question's resolution cutoff." ✓

### Double-Counting Check
Factors 1 and 2 both involve insider claims but address distinct aspects (timing vs. content credibility/track record). Factors 1 and 8 both touch on Direct timing but Factor 8 introduces a contrarian data point (June 16). No material double-counting.

### Wrong-Direction Check
All logit signs are consistent with factor descriptions. Positive factors support YES, negative factors support NO. ✓

### Contradictory Assessment Check
The assessment correctly decomposes P(YES) = P(Direct before June 14) × P(OoT|Direct) + P(other channel), yielding ~44%. The agent recognizes that the factor logit sum implies ~61% but correctly explains that the conditional decomposition is more rigorous for AND-structured questions. The agent trusts the decomposition over the factor sum. This is methodologically sound. ✓

### Counterargument Quality
Strong. The bull case constructs a specific alternative (P(Direct)=87% × P(OoT|Direct)=67% = 58%) with concrete reasoning. The bear case identifies June 16 slippage risk. Specific "what would change my mind" triggers are listed. Not token. ✓

### Confidence Self-Assessment
0.40 is appropriate given all evidence is from insider rumors (no official confirmation), the question requires two uncertain conditions to be jointly met, and the resolution window is tight. ✓

### Probability Assessment
My independent estimate: P(Direct before June 14) ≈ 75-80%, P(OoT|Direct) ≈ 50-55%, P(other) ≈ 2-3%. Combined: ~40-48%. The agent's 44% falls squarely within my range. No meaningful divergence.

### Anchor Divergence
Anchor of 35-40% with final estimate of 44% — a modest 4-9pp departure justified by the specific convergence of multiple credible insiders on a date within the window. Reasonable.

### Tool Audit
Agent claims one research query lacked real-time search capability. Ground truth confirms: the second research query returned "All web search and web fetch tools are unavailable in this session." Claims of 0 errors across web_search (5 calls) and fetch_url (4 calls) match ground truth metrics. ✓

### Worldview/Cross-Question Consistency
Prior Nintendo-related forecasts (43535, 43544, 43556) concern Switch 2 hardware sales and stock prices — unrelated to OoT announcement. No contradictions found. No numeric sub-forecast exists for this binary question's underlying quantity.

### Overall
All factual claims are grounded in the research trace. The conditional probability decomposition is methodologically sound. The 44% estimate is well-calibrated given the evidence. No errors found.
